apiVersion: powerloom.app/v2
kind: Agent
metadata:
  name: fact-checker
  ou_path: /media-co/editorial
spec:
  display_name: Fact Checker
  description: Reviews editorial drafts before publication and flags claims requiring citations. Prioritizes accuracy for published journalism.
  model: claude-opus-4-20250514
  system_prompt: |
    You are a fact-checking agent for a media organization. Your role is to review editorial content before publication and identify claims that require citations or verification.

    For each draft you review:
    1. Read the entire piece carefully
    2. Identify factual claims, statistics, quotes, and assertions
    3. Flag any claim that would benefit from a citation or source
    4. Distinguish between opinion/analysis (no citation needed) and factual claims (citation required)
    5. Note claims that may be controversial or easily disputed
    6. Provide specific guidance on what type of source would be appropriate

    Output your findings as a structured list with:
    - The claim text (quote it exactly)
    - Why it needs a citation
    - Suggested source type (academic paper, official statistics, expert interview, etc.)
    - Urgency level (critical/important/nice-to-have)

    Prioritize accuracy and credibility. When in doubt, flag for citation.
  runtime_type: cma
  agent_kind: user
  owner_principal_ref: user:editor@media-co.com
  skills: []
  mcp_servers: []
  coordinator_role: false
  task_kinds:
    - qa
  memory_permissions: []
  reranker_model: null

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v2
kind: Agent
metadata:
  name: fact-checker
  ou_path: /media-co/editorial
spec:
  display_name: Fact Checker
  description: Reviews editorial drafts and flags claims requiring citations before publication
  model: claude-opus-4-20250514
  system_prompt: |
    You are a fact-checking agent for a media organization. Your role is to review editorial drafts before publication and identify claims that require citations.

    For each draft you review:
    1. Read the entire piece carefully
    2. Identify factual claims, statistics, quotes, and assertions
    3. Flag any claim that would benefit from a citation or source
    4. Distinguish between:
       - Common knowledge (no citation needed)
       - Opinion/analysis (no citation needed)
       - Factual claims requiring verification
    5. Provide specific guidance on what type of source would be appropriate

    Output your findings as a structured list with:
    - The claim text (quote from draft)
    - Why it needs a citation
    - Suggested source type (academic, government data, expert interview, etc.)

    Prioritize accuracy and thoroughness over speed. Published journalism requires high standards of verification.
  runtime_type: cma
  agent_kind: user
  owner_principal_ref: user:editor@media-co.com
  skills: []
  mcp_servers: []
  coordinator_role: false
  task_kinds:
    - qa
  memory_permissions: []
  reranker_model: null

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v2
kind: Agent
metadata:
  name: fact-checker
  ou_path: /media-co/editorial
spec:
  display_name: Fact Checker
  description: Reviews editorial drafts and flags claims requiring citations before publication
  model: claude-opus-4-20250514
  system_prompt: |
    You are a fact-checking agent for a media organization. Your role is to review editorial drafts before publication and identify claims that require citations.

    For each draft you review:
    1. Read the entire piece carefully
    2. Identify factual claims, statistics, quotes, and assertions
    3. Flag any claim that would benefit from a citation or source
    4. Distinguish between:
       - Common knowledge (no citation needed)
       - Opinions clearly attributed to the author (no citation needed)
       - Factual claims requiring verification (citation needed)
       - Statistics and data points (citation always needed)
       - Direct quotes from sources (attribution needed)

    For each flagged claim, provide:
    - The exact text of the claim
    - Why it needs a citation
    - Suggested source types (academic, government data, expert interview, etc.)
    - Severity: critical (must have before publication) or recommended (strengthens credibility)

    Be thorough but practical. The goal is journalistic integrity without creating unnecessary burden.
  runtime_type: cma
  agent_kind: user
  owner_principal_ref: user:editor@media-co.com
  skills: []
  mcp_servers: []
  coordinator_role: false
  task_kinds:
    - qa
  memory_permissions: []
  reranker_model: null

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v1

          description: Author's location (optional)
      additionalProperties: false
  system: true
  auto_attach_to:
    task_kinds:
      - execution

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v1
kind: Skill
metadata:
  name: bloom-taxonomy-labeler
  ou_path: /edu-co/curriculum
spec:
  display_name: Bloom's Taxonomy Labeler
  description: Labels questions with Bloom's taxonomy cognitive level (Remember, Understand, Apply, Analyze, Evaluate, Create). Enables QA agents to assess question depth and learning objectives.
  skill_type: tool_definition
  tool_schema:
    name: label_bloom_level
    description: Analyzes a question or prompt and returns its Bloom's taxonomy cognitive level
    input_schema:
      type: object
      required:
        - question_text
      properties:
        question_text:
          type: string
          description: The question or prompt to analyze
        context:
          type: string
          description: Optional context about the learning domain or subject matter
      additionalProperties: false
    output_schema:
      type: object
      required:
        - bloom_level
        - confidence
      properties:
        bloom_level:
          type: string
          enum:
            - Remember
            - Understand
            - Apply
            - Analyze
            - Evaluate
            - Create
          description: The identified Bloom's taxonomy level
        confidence:
          type: number
          minimum: 0
          maximum: 1
          description: Confidence score for the classification
        reasoning:
          type: string
          description: Brief explanation of why this level was assigned
  system: true
  auto_attach_to:
    task_kinds:
      - qa

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v1
kind: Skill
metadata:
  name: var-calculator
  ou_path: /finance-co/risk
spec:
  display_name: VaR Calculator
  description: Calculates Value-at-Risk for a portfolio snapshot using historical simulation and parametric methods.
  skill_type: tool_definition
  tool_schema:
    name: calculate_var
    description: Computes Value-at-Risk (VaR) at specified confidence levels for a given portfolio position set.
    input_schema:
      type: object
      required:
        - positions
        - confidence_level
        - time_horizon_days
      properties:
        positions:
          type: array
          description: Array of portfolio positions with ticker and quantity.
          items:
            type: object
            required:
              - ticker
              - quantity
            properties:
              ticker:
                type: string
              quantity:
                type: number
        confidence_level:
          type: number
          description: Confidence level for VaR calculation (e.g., 0.95 for 95%).
          minimum: 0.5
          maximum: 0.999
        time_horizon_days:
          type: integer
          description: Time horizon in days for VaR calculation.
          minimum: 1
          maximum: 252
        method:
          type: string
          enum:
            - historical
            - parametric
            - monte_carlo
          default: historical
  system: true
  auto_attach_to:
    agent_kinds:
      - service
    task_kinds:
      - analogy

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v2
kind: Compose
metadata:
  name: IncidentRunbook
  namespace: ops.acme
  display_name: Incident Runbook
  description: A composed kind for incident response runbooks that extends Process and adds runtime policy constraints
spec:
  compose:
    - extends: Process
      role: runbook_template
      fields:
        process_kind: incident_runbook
        inputs_schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            incident_id:
              type: string
            severity:
              type: string
              enum: [critical, high, medium, low]
            description:
              type: string
          required: [incident_id, severity]
        outputs_schema:
          type: object
          properties:
            resolution_status:
              type: string
              enum: [resolved, escalated, pending]
            actions_taken:
              type: array
              items:
                type: string
          required: [resolution_status]
    - primitive: Policy
      role: runtime
      fields:
        policy_type: runtime
        applies_to:
          - ops.acme
        statement:
          runtime_type:
            - cma
            - anthropic_messages
        enforcement_mode: enforce
  interface:
    process_kind:
      type: string
      const: incident_runbook
    inputs_schema:
      type: object
    outputs_schema:
      type: object
    runtime_constraints:
      type: object
      properties:
        allowed_runtimes:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v2
kind: Compose
metadata:
  name: SupportEscalation
  namespace: support.acme
  display_name: Support Escalation
  description: Composed kind for support escalation workflows with tier-based scoping and escalation rule policies
spec:
  compose:
    - extends: WorkflowType
      role: workflow_template
      fields:
        task_kinds:
          - routing
          - coordination
        runtime_targets:
          - cma
    - primitive: Scope
      role: escalation_tier
      fields:
        scope_type: custom
        inheritance_mode: selective
        selective_inheritance:
          memory_kinds:
            - procedural
            - concepts
    - primitive: Policy
      role: escalation_rules
      fields:
        policy_type: escalation_rules
        enforcement_mode: enforce
        statement:
          max_tier: 3
          auto_escalate_after_hours: 24
          require_approval_above_tier: 2
  interface:
    tier_level:
      type: integer
      minimum: 1
      maximum: 3
    escalation_criteria:
      type: object
      properties:
        severity:
          type: string
          enum: [low, medium, high, critical]
        customer_tier:
          type: string
          enum: [free, paid, enterprise]
    coordinator_agent_id:
      type: string
      format: uuid
  constraints:
    - exactly-one-Policy-slot-with-role-escalation_rules
    - Scope-slot-must-have-custom-scope_type

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v2
kind: Compose
metadata:
  name: SupportEscalation
  namespace: support.acme
  display_name: Support Escalation
  description: Composed kind for support escalation workflows with tier-based scoping and escalation rule policies
spec:
  compose:
    - extends: WorkflowType
      role: workflow_template
      fields:
        task_kinds:
          - routing
          - escalation
        runtime_targets:
          - cma
    - primitive: Scope
      role: escalation_tier
      fields:
        scope_type: custom
        inheritance_mode: selective
        selective_inheritance:
          memory_kinds:
            - procedural
            - concepts
    - primitive: Policy
      role: escalation_rules
      fields:
        policy_type: escalation
        enforcement_mode: enforce
        statement:
          max_tier: 3
          auto_escalate_after_hours: 24
          require_approval_at_tier: 2
  interface:
    tier_level:
      type: integer
      minimum: 1
      maximum: 3
    escalation_criteria:
      type: object
      properties:
        severity:
          type: string
          enum: [low, medium, high, critical]
        response_time_sla_hours:
          type: integer
    coordinator_agent_id:
      type: string
      format: uuid
  constraints:
    - exactly-one-scope-slot-with-role-escalation_tier
    - exactly-one-policy-slot-with-role-escalation_rules
    - workflow-template-must-have-coordinator

apiVersion: powerloom.app/v2
kind: Compose
metadata:
  name: SupportEscalation
  namespace: support.acme
  display_name: Support Escalation
  description: Composed kind for support ticket escalation workflows with tier-based scoping and escalation rule policies
spec:
  compose:
    - extends: WorkflowType
      role: workflow_template
      fields:
        task_kinds:
          - routing
          - escalation
        runtime_targets:
          - cma
    - primitive: Scope
      role: escalation_tier
      fields:
        scope_type: custom
        inheritance_mode: selective
        selective_inheritance:
          from_types:
            - support_ticket
            - customer_context
          memory_kinds:
            - procedural
            - concepts
    - primitive: Policy
      role: escalation_rules
      fields:
        policy_type: escalation
        enforcement_mode: enforce
        statement:
          max_tier: 3
          auto_escalate_after_hours: 24
          require_manager_approval: true
          escalation_criteria:
            - severity: critical
              tier: 3
            - severity: high
              tier: 2
            - severity: medium
              tier: 1
  interface:
    tier_level:
      type: integer
      minimum: 1
      maximum: 3
    escalation_path:
      type: array
      items:
        type: string
    approval_required:
      type: boolean
  constraints:
    - exactly-one-scope-with-role-escalation_tier
    - policy-escalation_rules-must-define-statement.escalation_criteria
